items.mutated_body: Currently the connector hard‑codes its configuration inside the `CruxOMRSRepositoryConnector` class. We should really allow the configuration to be passed in as part of the admin services that configure the connection itself.

items.original_title: Unity UWP app using AWS .NET SDK functions fails with COM/Il2CppExceptionWrapper exceptions
items.original_body: I've imported the .NET SDK **V3.3** DLLs and the Amazon Cognito Authentication Extension DLL into a Unity 2020.1 project and created an app that authenticates with Cognito and then accesses a DynamoDB table. I cannot currently use the latest .NET SDK version 3.5 in this Unity app due to #1722 . The Unity app works fine in the Unity Editor and when built as a PC Standalone app. However when it is built in Unity and executed as a UWP app it fails, generated many COM "class not registered" exceptions, followed by many "Il2CppExceptionWrapper at memory location" exceptions before terminating. I did construct a Unity linker file to prevent assembly stripping as outlined [here](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/developer/referencing-the-aws-sdk-for-net-standard-2-0-from-unity-xamarin-or-uwp/).\r\n\r\nAny ideas or advice on why this is happening would be very welcome.
items.mutated_title: The Unity app works in the Unity Editor and when built as a PC Standalone app.
items.mutated_body: I've imported the .NET SDK **V3.3** DLLs and the Amazon Cognito Authentication Extension DLL into a Unity 2020.1 project and created an app that authenticates with Cognito and then accesses a DynamoDB table. I cannot currently use the latest .NET SDK version 3.5 in this Unity app due to #1722 . The Unity app works fine in the Unity Editor and when built as a PC Standalone app. However when it is built in Unity and executed as a UWP app it fails, generated many COM "class not registered" exceptions, followed by many "Il2CppExceptionWrapper at memory location" exceptions before terminating. I did construct a Unity linker file to prevent assembly stripping as outlined [here](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/developer/referencing-the-aws-sdk-for-net-standard-2-0-from-unity-xamarin-or-uwp/).\r\n\r\nAny ideas or advice on why this is happening would be very welcome.

items.original_body: With the current React Routing, the default landing page URL is at '/', which is currently undefined (as in no component is instructed to render when user is on that page)\r\n\r\nTo address this issue, a Welcome component can be implemented. This component could also act as a tutorial for the user, instructing them on how to access various pages from the menu.
items.mutated_body: With the current React Routing, the default landing page URL is at '/', which is undefined (as in no component is instructed to render when user is on that page). To address this issue, a Welcome component can be implemented. This component could also act as a tutorial for the user, instructing them on how to access various pages from the menu.

items.original_body:
We are using a Gitlab installation that requires a separate keytab file which must be readable by the "git" user (see https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/integration/kerberos.html). Of course we can copy the existing /etc/krb5.keytab to the desired path and adjust the permissions. However, this will work for some time and then break because the krb5.keytab gets frequently updated by msktutil.

I am looking for a way to update the service keytab via msktutil as well. Is that possible with the current option set?

PS: We can copy the krb5.keytab via cron but then the service keytab is not restricted to the service principals but contains _all_ principals which I would like to avoid. I tried to extract the relevant keys from the krb5.keytab and feed them to ktutil but that tool is not cooperative when it comes to passing keys via stdin...
items.mutated_body: We are using a Gitlab installation that requires a separate file which must be readable by the "git" user (see https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/integration/kerberos.html). Of course we can copy the existing /etc/krb5. to the desired path and adjust the permissions. However, this will work for some time and then break because the krb5. gets frequently updated by msktutil. I am looking for a way to update the service via msktutil as well. Is that possible with the current option set? PS: We can copy the krb5. via cron but then the service is not restricted to the service principals but contains _all_ principals which I would like to avoid. I tried to extract the relevant keys from the krb5. and feed them to ktutil but that tool is not cooperative when it comes to passing keys via stdin...

items.original_body: Currently, run-item matching is performed with fnmatch, which is useful for strings. Add '<' and '>' range matching that is useful for numerical datatypes (e.g. EchoTime: ">80<100")
items.mutated_body: Currently, run-item matching is performed with fnmatch, which is useful for strings. Add '<' and '>' range matching that is useful for numerical datatypes (e.g. EchoTime: ">80<100")

items.original_body: When I open the app, it immediately crashes with a TypeError. Expected behavior: the app should show the home screen.
items.mutated_body: When I open the application, it immediately crashes with a TypeError. Expected behavior: the home screen should be shown.
